q: A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions.
h:
“A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions.” —
Marcus Aurelius

q: Shoot for the moon, because even if you miss, you'll land among the stars.
h:
“Shoot for the moon, because even if you miss, you'll land among the stars.” —
Les Brown

q: When you lose your desire for things that do not matter, you will be free.
h:
“When you lose your desire for things that do not matter, you will be free.” —
Morihei Ueshiba

q: No great mind has ever existed without a touch of madness.
h:
“No great mind has ever existed without a touch of madness.” —
Aristotle

q: To live outside the law, you must be honest.
h:
“To live outside the law, you must be honest. ” —
Bob Dylan

q: Pleasure is always derived from something outside you, whereas joy arises from within.
h:
“Pleasure is always derived from something outside you, whereas joy arises from within. ” —
Eckhart Tolle

q: Man is affected not by events but by the view he takes of them.
h:
“Man is affected not by events but by the view he takes of them. ” —
Seneca

q: He who knows how to wait need make no concessions.
h:
“He who knows how to wait need make no concessions.” —
Sigmund Freud

q: A man should never be ashamed to own that he has been in the wrong, which is but saying, that he is wiser today than yesterday.
h:
“A man should never be ashamed to own that he has been in the wrong, which is but saying, that he is wiser today than yesterday.” —
Jonathan Swift

q: Don't waste your energy on being angry at something that somebody did six months ago or a year ago. It's over.
h:
“Don't waste your energy on being angry at something that somebody did six months ago or a year ago. It's over.” —
Joan Rivers

q: It takes half your life before you discover life is a do-it-yourself project.
h:
“It takes half your life before you discover life is a do-it-yourself project.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: To judge a man by his weakest link or deed is like judging the power of the ocean by one wave.
h:
“To judge a man by his weakest link or deed is like judging the power of the ocean by one wave.” —
Elvis Presley

q: Be - don't try to become
h:
“Be - don't try to become ” —
Osho

q: The secret of happiness, you see, is not found in seeking more, but in developing the capacity to enjoy less.
h:
“The secret of happiness, you see, is not found in seeking more, but in developing the capacity to enjoy less.” —
Dan Millman

q: There is a big difference between being centered and being self-centered.
h:
“There is a big difference between being centered and being self-centered.” —
Lolly Daskal

q: Go and do the things you can't. That is how you get to do them.
h:
“Go and do the things you can't. That is how you get to do them.” —
Pablo Picasso

q: We must be free not because we claim freedom, but because we practice it.
h:
“We must be free not because we claim freedom, but because we practice it.” —
William Faulkner

q: Life is not a problem to be solved, but a reality to be experienced.
h:
“Life is not a problem to be solved, but a reality to be experienced.” —
Soren Kierkegaard

q: There's a very deep connection among human beings. All we have to do is open our minds to it.
h:
“There's a very deep connection among human beings. All we have to do is open our minds to it.” —
Yanni

q: The purpose of life is the expansion of happiness.
h:
“The purpose of life is the expansion of happiness.” —
Deepak Chopra

q: It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live, remember that.
h:
“It does not do to dwell on dreams and forget to live, remember that.” —
Albus Dumbledore

q: Avoiding danger is no safer in the long run than exposure.
h:
“Avoiding danger is no safer in the long run than exposure.” —
Helen Keller

q: Forget safety. Live where you fear to live.
h:
“Forget safety. Live where you fear to live.” —
Rumi

q: The golden opportunity you are seeking is within yourself.
h:
“The golden opportunity you are seeking is within yourself.” —
Mary Engelbreit

q: Ideas do not reach perfection in a day, no matter how much study is put upon them.
h:
“Ideas do not reach perfection in a day, no matter how much study is put upon them.” —
Alexander Graham Bell

q: People inspire you, or they drain you. Pick them wisely.
h:
“People inspire you, or they drain you. Pick them wisely.” —
Les Brown

q: Courage is not having the strength to go on; it is going on when you don't have the strength.
h:
“Courage is not having the strength to go on; it is going on when you don't have the strength.” —
Theodore Roosevelt

q: Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you've imagined.
h:
“Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you've imagined.” —
Arnold Schwarzenegger

q: Turn off your mind, relax, and float downstream.
h:
“Turn off your mind, relax, and float downstream.” —
John Lennon

q: A mentor is someone who sees more talent and ability within you, than you see in yourself, and helps bring it out of you.
h:
“A mentor is someone who sees more talent and ability within you, than you see in yourself, and helps bring it out of you.” —
Bob Proctor

q: The problem happens when we have multiple desires. When we have fuzzy desires. When we want to do ten different things and we're not clear about which is the one we care about.
h:
“The problem happens when we have multiple desires. When we have fuzzy desires. When we want to do ten different things and we're not clear about which is the one we care about.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: The degree of responsibility you take for your life determines how much change you can create in it.
h:
“The degree of responsibility you take for your life determines how much change you can create in it.” —
Celestine Chua

q: When you are tempted to give up, your breakthrough is probably just around the corner.
h:
“When you are tempted to give up, your breakthrough is probably just around the corner.” —
Joyce Meyer

q: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
h:
“Believe you can and you're halfway there.” —
Theodore Roosevelt

q: There are as many opinions as there are experts.
h:
“There are as many opinions as there are experts.” —
Franklin D. Roosevelt

q: Absurdity is the ecstasy of intellectualism.
h:
“Absurdity is the ecstasy of intellectualism.” —
Criss Jami

q: I do not exist to impress the world. I exist to live my life in a way that will make me happy.
h:
“I do not exist to impress the world. I exist to live my life in a way that will make me happy.” —
Richard Bach

q: A fear-oriented man is always calculating, planning, arranging, safeguarding. His whole life is lost in this way.
h:
“A fear-oriented man is always calculating, planning, arranging, safeguarding. His whole life is lost in this way.” —
Osho

q: Act the way you want to be and soon you'll be the way you act.
h:
“Act the way you want to be and soon you'll be the way you act.” —
Les Brown

q: Fools resist. The wise embrace.
h:
“Fools resist. The wise embrace.” —
Maxime Lagace

q: There are no second chances in life, except to feel remorse.
h:
“There are no second chances in life, except to feel remorse.” —
Carlos Ruiz Zafon

q: The only limits in our life are those we impose on ourselves.
h:
“The only limits in our life are those we impose on ourselves.” —
Bob Proctor

q: Nothing brings down walls as surely as acceptance.
h:
“Nothing brings down walls as surely as acceptance.” —
Deepak Chopra

q: The best way to understanding is a few good examples.
h:
“The best way to understanding is a few good examples.” —
Isaac Newton

q: Don't settle, and don't struggle.
h:
“Don't settle, and don't struggle.” —
Naval Ravikant

q: Whatever your mind can conceive and believe, it can achieve.
h:
“Whatever your mind can conceive and believe, it can achieve.” —
Napoleon Hill

q: A little progress each day adds up to big results.
h:
“A little progress each day adds up to big results.” —
Unknown

q: Fallacies do not cease to be fallacies because they become fashions.
h:
“Fallacies do not cease to be fallacies because they become fashions.” —
Gilbert Chesterton

q: The person who cannot control his words shows that he cannot control himself.
h:
“The person who cannot control his words shows that he cannot control himself.” —
Robert Greene

q: Whatever satisfies the soul is truth.
h:
“Whatever satisfies the soul is truth.” —
Walt Whitman
